messages.author: solohook (LUSK, BOOG, BBBR, 🪝)
messages.text: Hello! I'm actually on the Illinois side of STL and wanting to get MeshCore built out. I have put up a temporary repeater and have some companion nodes. There are a few other folks in the area wanting to give MC a go as well. Just wanted to post something in hopes someone in the area was wondering if there was any intrest in MC. Happy meshing and Happy New Year ya'll. (edited)
messages.content_html: Hello! I'm actually on the Illinois side of STL and wanting to get MeshCore built out. I have put up a temporary repeater and have some companion nodes. Happy meshing and Happy New Year ya'll. (edited)

messages.author: solohook (LUSK, BOOG, BBBR, 🪝)
messages.text: Finally caught someone! I switched my tiny alfa out for AliExpress antenna today. Great! But I forgot to put vulcanizing tape back on the connector and rain is forecast for this afternoon. Oops

messages.author: supaflys
messages.text: In STL Lindbergh corridor setting up MC repeaters at Saint Charles Rock Road & Lindbergh and Olive & Lindbergh. Noticing the US preset 910.525mhz has a higher noise floor in 90's db in this area than the 927.825mhz SoCal uses which is 105-110db here. Since I am mainly doing this for family comms I might switch to what will work better for us. Going to test a bit and see which channel has the best range. (edited)
messages.content_html: In STL Lindbergh corridor setting up MC repeaters at Saint Charles Rock Road & Lindbergh and Olive & Lindbergh. Noticing the US preset 910.525mhz has a higher noise floor in 90's db in this area than the 927.825mhz SoCal uses which is 105-110db here. Since I am mainly doing this for family comms I might switch to what will work better for us. Going to test a bit and see which channel has the best range. (edited)

messages.author: supaflys
messages.text: Testing confirmed. 927.825 mhz is a much cleaner band in Saint Louis. Noise floor is 10-15db better both north and south and resulting in longer range comms. One wonders if all the people who switch from MT to MC briefly and use the preset are just having shitty experiences due to this one bad preset fits all for the US. (edited)
messages.content_html: Testing confirmed. 927.825 mhz is a much cleaner band in Saint Louis. Noise floor is 10-15db better both north and south and resulting in longer range comms. messages.author: solohook (LUSK, BOOG, BBBR, 🪝)
messages.text: I just swapped out my tiny alfa on rooftop v4 repeater for an amazon special. It claims 5dbi. Edwardsville currently flooding the abyss.

messages.author: Uncle Lit
messages.text: the meshcore dev team have moved to a new discord server. details and reasons are here: https://blog.meshcore.io/2026/04/23/the-split Please create a new thread for your region in the https://discord.com/channels/1495203904898728149/1495412453150818585 for your region on the new meshcore.io discord server. https://meshcore.gg
